_comment: Create flow — right rail / decision approaches (Figma Flow — Right Rail `20523:23509`)

sidebar.title: How should this community make difficult decisions?
sidebar.descriptionBefore: Select as many as you need to describe how your group makes decisions. You can also
sidebar.descriptionAfter: new decision making approaches or interact with the categories below to filter.

messageBox.title: Consider defining approaches to steward key resources:

lazy-consensus.title: Lazy Consensus
lazy-consensus.description: A decision is assumed approved unless objections are raised within a specified timeframe.

sections.corePrinciple: We prioritize momentum and trust over bureaucracy. By assuming good faith, we avoid bottlenecks; silence is interpreted as consent to keep the work moving.
sections.stepByStepInstructions: Post your proposal to the relevant channel with a specific deadline, such as 'Merging in 72 hours.' If the deadline passes without any objections, you are authorized to proceed. Explicit support is welcome but not required.
sections.objectionsDeadlocks: Any member can pause the process by raising a 'Block' or 'Concern' before the deadline. The proposer is then required to pause execution and engage in a dialogue to resolve the concern. If the disagreement cannot be resolved asynchronously, the proposal is escalated to a synchronous meeting or a higher governance tier for a final decision.

cards.label: Lazy Consensus
cards.supportText: A decision is assumed approved unless objections are raised within a specified timeframe.

cards.label: Do-ocracy
cards.supportText: Decisions are made by those who take initiative and carry out the work.

cards.label: Consensus Decision-Making
cards.supportText: All members must agree. Best for important decisions in small groups. Does not work well for low stakes decisions.

cards.label: Modified Consensus
cards.supportText: Attempts to reach full agreement first, but falls back to voting if consensus isn’t possible.
